name: Aglianico
profile: Powerful, age-worthy, high tannin, high acidity. Notes: black cherry, leather, tar, smoke.
pairings: Braised meats (short ribs, lamb shank), grilled steak, lasagna, mushroom risotto, aged cheeses.
styles: Taurasi (The Barolo of the South), Aglianico del Vulture.

name: Albariño
profile: Crisp, saline, high acid. Notes: stone fruit, citrus, sea spray, honeysuckle.
pairings: Seafood (oysters, mussels), ceviche, grilled fish, salads with citrus vinaigrette.
note: Best enjoyed young and chilled.

name: Arneis
profile: Aromatic, textured, soft acidity. Notes: pear, white peach, flowers, almond finish.
pairings: Seafood, white fish (sole, halibut), creamy pasta, roast chicken, soft cheeses.

name: Barbera
profile: Vibrant acidity, juicy red fruit, low tannin. Notes: red cherry, plum, blackberry, dried herbs.
pairings: Pizza, tomato-based pasta, lasagna, burgers, roast chicken.

name: Beaujolais / Gamay
profile: Vibrant, aromatic, light-to-medium body. Notes: strawberry, raspberry, floral, spice.
pairings: Roast chicken, charcuterie, burgers, pizza, grilled salmon.
styles: 10 Crus (e.g., Morgon, Fleurie, Moulin-à-Vent), Beaujolais Nouveau.

name: Bordeaux Regions
left_bank: Cabernet Sauvignon dominant, gravelly soil, structured/tannic (Margaux, Pauillac).
right_bank: Merlot dominant, clay-limestone soil, plush/velvety (Saint-Émilion, Pomerol).
pairings: Left Bank: Red meats, aged cheese. Right Bank: Poultry, stews.

name: Brachetto
profile: Sweet, aromatic, lightly sparkling red. Notes: rose petals, strawberry, raspberry, exotic spice.
pairings: Chocolate desserts, strawberry shortcake, fruit salad, spicy Asian dishes.
note: The 'King of aromatic reds'.

name: Cabernet Franc
profile: Medium-bodied, herbaceous, floral. Notes: red fruit, graphite, green bell pepper, tobacco.
pairings: Pork tenderloin, roast chicken, duck breast, herb-driven dishes, goat cheese.
lineage: Parent grape of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot.

name: Cabernet Sauvignon
profile: Full-bodied, high tannin, moderate acidity. Notes: blackcurrant, blackberry, cedar, tobacco.
pairings: Steak, lamb, aged cheddar, rich protein-heavy dishes.
note: Foundation of the global wine industry.

name: Carignan
profile: Deeply colored, high-acid, high-tannin. Notes: blackberry, plum, baking spices, earth.
pairings: BBQ, roast meats, Mediterranean cuisine, rustic stews (Cassoulet).
note: Revived old-vine classic.

name: Carménère
profile: Deeply colored, aromatic, herb-tinged. Notes: blackberry, plum, green pepper, tobacco.
pairings: Grilled meats, Mexican cuisine (tacos, mole), Indian curries, roasted vegetables.
note: Sitting between Merlot and Cabernet Franc stylistically.

name: Cava
profile: Traditional method sparkling. Notes: green apple, citrus, almond, brioche.
pairings: Tapas, seafood (paella), fried foods (calamari), Manchego cheese.

name: Chardonnay
profile: Versatile. Cool climate: crisp, mineral (Chablis). Warm climate: buttery, oaky (California).
pairings: Unoaked: seafood, salads. Oaked: roasted chicken, creamy pasta, lobster.
note: Reflects winemaking technique more than any other grape.

name: Chenin Blanc
profile: High acidity, diverse styles (dry to sweet/sparkling). Notes: apple, honey, minerals.
pairings: Seafood, poultry, goat cheese, spicy Thai or Indian dishes.
note: Highly sensitive to terroir.

name: Crémant
profile: Traditional method sparkling made outside Champagne. Often fruit-forward and creamy.
pairings: Aperitifs, brunch, fried chicken, soft cheeses.
note: Excellent value alternative to Champagne.

name: Dolcetto
profile: Dry, dark-fruited, soft tannins, low acidity. Notes: black cherry, blackberry, licorice, almond.
pairings: Pizza, pasta with meat sauce, charcuterie, burgers.
nickname: Little sweet one (refers to grape sweetness, though wine is dry).

name: Gewürztraminer
profile: Intensely aromatic, full-bodied, low acid. Notes: lychee, rose petal, ginger, spice.
pairings: Thai and Indian curries, Moroccan tagines, Roquefort cheese, foie gras.
note: One of the most distinctive and polarizing grapes.

name: Grenache / Garnacha
profile: Warm climate, red berries, spice, high alcohol. Notes: strawberry, raspberry, white pepper.
pairings: Grilled/roasted meats, stews, Mediterranean dishes, tapas.
note: Main component of GSM blends.

name: Grüner Veltliner
profile: Bright acidity, signature white-pepper spice. Notes: lime, green apple, fresh herbs.
pairings: Wiener Schnitzel, asparagus (rare pairing), spicy Asian dishes, pork.
note: Uniquely food-friendly white.

name: Malbec
profile: Full-bodied, dark-fruited, velvety. Notes: blackberry, blueberry, violet, smoke.
pairings: Steak with chimichurri, BBQ, ribs, burgers, earthy dishes (mushrooms).
note: Known for its plush texture and deep purple color.

name: Merlot
profile: Plush, velvety, fruit-forward. Notes: plum, black cherry, cocoa, mocha.
pairings: Roasted chicken, pork tenderloin, burgers, beef stew, Gouda cheese.
note: Cornerstone of Right Bank Bordeaux.

name: Moscato
profile: Highly aromatic, floral, fruity. Notes: orange blossom, peach, apricot, honey.
pairings: Brunch dishes, fruit tarts, spicy Asian cuisine, Indian curries.

name: Mourvèdre / Monastrell
profile: Full-bodied, tannic, gamey. Notes: black pepper, smoke, wild game, dark fruit.
pairings: Lamb, venison, smoked brisket, hearty stews, Manchego cheese.
note: Key in GSM blends, known for power and rustic charm.

name: Muscadet
profile: Dry, crisp, mineral, saline. Notes: lemon, lime, oyster shell, sea spray.
pairings: Oysters (the ultimate match), mussels, clams, shrimp, sushi.
note: Often aged 'sur lie' for added texture.

name: Nebbiolo
profile: Noble grape, very high tannin/acid, pale color. Notes: tar, roses, cherry, truffle, earth.
pairings: Truffle dishes, braised meats (osso buco), ribeye steak, aged cheeses.
styles: Barolo (King of Wines), Barbaresco (Queen of Wines).

name: Petite Sirah / Durif
profile: Deeply colored, massive tannins, bold fruit. Notes: blueberry, black pepper, cocoa.
pairings: BBQ and smoked meats, grilled steak, bison, rich cheeses (aged cheddar).
note: Mislabeled in California, actually the grape Durif.

name: Petit Verdot
profile: Deeply colored, intensely structured. Notes: violet, black fruit, graphite, exotic spice.
pairings: Grilled steak, BBQ, lamb chops, black pepper-crusted dishes.
note: Used in blends for color and structure, but great as a standalone New World varietal.

name: Pinot Blanc
profile: Elegant, fresh, subtly aromatic. Notes: apple, pear, almond, gentle florals.
pairings: Seafood (trout, scallops), roast chicken, risotto, soft cheeses.
note: Understated and refined alternative to Chardonnay.

name: Pinot Grigio / Pinot Gris
profile: Chameleon white. Italian (PG): light, crisp, mineral. Alsatian (PG): rich, oily, spicy.
pairings: Italian style: shellfish, salads. French style: roast duck, sausages, spicy curries.
note: One of the most widely consumed whites.

name: Pinot Noir
profile: Cool-climate, silky, terroir-driven. Notes: cherry, raspberry, mushroom, forest floor.
pairings: Duck, roast chicken, pork, grilled salmon, mushroom risotto.
note: Benchmark for finesse and complexity.

name: Riesling
profile: Exceptional aromatic clarity, high acid. Notes: green apple, lime, slate, petrol (aged).
pairings: Dry: shellfish, herb-driven dishes. Off-dry: spicy Asian, foie gras, rich cheeses.
note: Covers the full spectrum from bone-dry to lusciously sweet.

name: Sangiovese
profile: High acid, firm tannin, savory. Notes: cherry, tomato leaf, herbs, tobacco, earth.
pairings: Tomato-based dishes (pizza, pasta), Florentine steak, roast pork, lamb chops.

name: Sauvignon Blanc
profile: High acidity, pungent aromatics. Notes: grapefruit, grass, gooseberry, flint.
pairings: Goat cheese (classic), shellfish, white fish, green vegetables (asparagus).
note: A palate cleanser that amplifies freshness.

name: Syrah / Shiraz
profile: Powerful, peppery. Rhone style: elegant, smoky. Australian style: bold, jammy, chocolate.
pairings: Grilled red meats, lamb, smoked sausages, spicy vegetarian fare.
note: Known for a distinct black pepper (rotundone) note.

name: Tempranillo
profile: Medium-to-full body, balanced acidity. Notes: cherry, plum, tobacco, leather, vanilla (aged).
pairings: Lamb, beef, chorizo, tomato-based dishes, aged cheeses (Manchego).

name: Valpolicella
profile: Vibrant and diverse. Notes: bright cherry, herbal, almond finish.
pairings: Classico: pizza, pasta. Ripasso/Amarone: short ribs, aged Parmigiano, dark chocolate.

name: Verdejo
profile: Crisp, citrus-driven, aromatic. Notes: lime, ginger, fennel, bitter-herbal finish.
pairings: Ceviche, fish tacos (lime-driven), salads, asparagus, fresh goat cheese.
note: Spain's most important white grape.

name: Verdicchio
profile: Crisp, mineral, high quality. Notes: citrus, fennel, saline, signature almond finish.
pairings: Grilled fish, oysters, pasta with seafood, roast chicken.
note: One of the few Italian whites capable of long aging.

name: Vernaccia
profile: Crisp, mineral, subtly floral. Notes: citrus, white peach, bitter almond finish.
pairings: Tuscan dishes (panzanella), light pasta with pesto, shellfish, pecorino.
note: Italy's first DOC wine (1966).

name: Vinho Verde
profile: Light, crisp, refreshing, low alcohol, subtle spritz. Notes: lime, green apple, floral.
pairings: Seafood, grilled fish, salads, spicy Thai/Vietnamese.
note: Translates to 'young wine', not green wine.

name: Viognier
profile: Highly aromatic, oily texture. Notes: peach, apricot, honeysuckle, exotic spice.
pairings: Spicy Indian or Thai curries, roast chicken with herbs, lobster, brie.
note: A high-risk, high-reward grape with luxurious texture.

name: Viura / Macabeo
profile: Versatile. Notes: citrus, green apple (young) to hazelnut, honey (aged).
pairings: Young: Southeast Asian, salads. Aged: Roast pork, grilled chicken.
note: A primary component of Cava and White Rioja.

name: Zinfandel
profile: Bold, jammy, fruit-forward, high alcohol. Notes: blackberry, raspberry, pepper, baking spice.
pairings: BBQ ribs, pulled pork, Burgers, chili, spicy veggie burgers.
note: Includes old-vine heritage and sweet 'White Zinfandel' styles.
